title: Understanding how builders have to navigate the regulatory health AI landscape (YouTube Short, FiCPH 2026)
snippet: Cora Han, JD, discusses the complexities of developers trying to navigate the regulatory health AI landscape during the 2026 Frontiers in Computational Precision Health conference.

title: Book Launch: "Data Consciousness Reframing Blackness in Contemporary Print" with Tiffany Barber, Safiya Noble, Silas Munro, and Randa Hadi
snippet: Book Launch event at UCLA DataX Impact Forum on June 2, 2026, featuring Safiya Noble alongside Tiffany Barber, Silas Munro, and Randa Hadi to discuss how technology and data relate to Blackness in contemporary print.
match_reason: websearch (talk) — Noble is listed as a named participant/speaker at this UCLA DataX book launch event touching on data, technology, and AI systems as they relate to race.

title: UCSB Emeriti Association Webinar: "Artificial Intelligence for Vulnerability Analysis: a Historical Perspective" with Giovanni Vigna
snippet: The UCSB Emeriti Association hosted a webinar with Giovanni Vigna, Distinguished Professor of Computer Science and Director of the NSF AI Institute for ACTION at UCSB, on June 4, 2026. The talk covered AI and LLMs applied to vulnerability analysis, tracing a historical arc from the DARPA Cyber Grand Challenge (2016) through the AI Cyber Challenge (AIxCC, 2025), discussing Cyber Reasoning Systems t
match_reason: websearch (talk) — Giovanni Vigna is the named speaker and subject of this UCSB Emeriti Association webinar on AI for vulnerability analysis.

title: SDSU faculty micro-credential advances responsible AI literacy in higher education
snippet: Peer-reviewed study in Journal of Information Systems Education finds SDSU's generative AI micro-credential increased faculty confidence and ethical engagement with AI tools. 374 faculty began; 98% would recommend it. Program expanded to CSU campuses and San Diego Community College District.
match_reason: websearch (news_article) — Empirical study on faculty AI readiness and responsible AI use in teaching at a California public university directly adjacent to the UC system.

title: AI for ImpaCT: UConn Launches University-Wide Initiative to Drive AI Innovation Across Connecticut
snippet: University of Connecticut launches a comprehensive AI initiative led by the provost's office covering teaching, research, workforce development and public engagement. A new AI Council with faculty, staff and student representation will be chaired by a Provost's Special Advisor on AI.
match_reason: websearch (press_release) — Peer R1 university governance model for AI integration in teaching and learning offers direct comparison for UC's steering committee charge.

title: "Building an AI-Ready America: Higher Education in the Age of AI" — House Subcommittee Hearing
snippet: The House Subcommittee on Higher Education and Workforce Development held its seventh AI hearing on June 3, examining how higher education can adapt students for an AI future. Witnesses included the CIO of Florida State University, McGraw Hill's Chief Product Officer, the CEO of the University Innovation Alliance, and a Harvard adjunct professor.
match_reason: websearch (other) — Federal legislative hearing on AI in higher education signals policy environment shaping UC governance and institutional AI strategy.
